response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned engineer (15+ years) with strong software-craftsmanship practices and proven experience building high-performance, event-driven, distributed systems (low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second, an event-driven geo-data platform). However, the role centers on modern C++/STL development and industrial-cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, SDL), and the resume shows only brief, dated C/C++ work (≈6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis). Core requirements such as recent C++/STL, MQTT/message brokers, and industrial protocols are largely absent, while his primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. He could be a fit if transferable systems and concurrency experience is weighted heavily, but he is not an obvious match for the C++-centric mandate.
strengths: Proven high-performance, low-latency distributed and event-driven systems experience
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development
Broad full-stack and cross-language background, including some C/C++ exposure
concerns: Minimal and dated hands-on C++/STL experience; primary stack is TS/Node and DLang
No evidence of IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial-cybersecurity practices
No demonstrated MQTT, message brokers, or industrial-automation protocol experience
